informal: Prove that if $\Omega=\{1,2,3, \ldots\}$ then $S_{\Omega}$ is an infinite group
reasoning: The informal statement asks to show that the symmetric group S_Ω on Ω = {1,2,3,...} is infinite. In Lean, `Equiv.Perm ℕ` is exactly the symmetric group on ℕ (the underlying set of permutations is the same as S_Ω, since {1,2,3,...} is in bijection with ℕ), and `Infinite` asserts that this type has infinitely many elements, which is precisely the claim that S_Ω is an infinite group. The group structure is implicit in `Equiv.Perm`, and "infinite group" in the informal sense just means the underlying set is infinite. No hypotheses are added or dropped.
